scope: Canadian-admitted barristers, solicitors, KCs/QCs, retired judges and legal academics who have handled PA cases at appellate level, published in the field, or testified/consulted in family-court PA matters. Stance is sourced, not inferred.

stance_taxonomy.recognition: treats PA as a legitimate, litigable phenomenon worth pursuing in family court
stance_taxonomy.critique: argues PA is misused in family court, especially against DV survivors
stance_taxonomy.middle: engages both family violence and alienating-behaviour frames; AFCC-aligned differentiated approach

practitioners.name: Harold Niman
practitioners.firm: Niman Counsel (in association with Gelgoot & Partners LLP), Toronto
bar_admissions: ON (Law Society of Ontario; year of call not publicly confirmed; 40+ years practice per profile)
named_cases: L.(A.G.) v D.(K.B.), 93 O.R. (3d) 409 (Ont. S.C.J. 2009) (McWatt J.) — lead counsel for applicant father
Editor and co-author, Evidence in Family Law textbook
Associate editor, Reports of Family Law
practitioners.hook: Lead counsel of record on the most-cited Ontario PA judgment (McWatt 2009) already in AntiAlienate's case-study set.
practitioners.notes: Predecessor firm Niman Gelgoot and Associates LLP at time of McWatt case. Niman Mamo LLP is a separate Niman-branded practice.

practitioners.name: Donna Wowk
bar_admissions: ON (Law Society of Ontario; 35+ years practice per profile; specific call year not publicly confirmed)
named_cases: L.(A.G.) v D.(K.B.), 2009 (McWatt J.) — co-counsel for applicant father (with Niman)
9-year service on Ontario Family Rules Committee
Conference speaker on parental alienation remedies; published articles on parental alienation per firm bio
practitioners.primary_contact: (647) 267-0847 ; 99 Yorkville Ave., Suite 200, Toronto, ON M5R 3K5
practitioners.hook: Junior counsel of record on McWatt 2009 — natural follow-on to Niman.
practitioners.notes: Previously at Niman Gelgoot and Associates LLP (12 years) and Harper Jaskot LLP.

practitioners.name: Brian Ludmer, B.Comm, LLB

practitioners.name: Suzanne Zaccour, BCL, LLB, LLM, DPhil (Oxon)
practitioners.firm: National Association of Women and the Law (NAWL / ANFD) — Director of Legal Affairs
bar_admissions: ON (Law Society of Ontario, called 2021); Supreme Court of Canada law clerk 2019-2020; BCL/LLB McGill; LLM Toronto; DPhil Oxford
named_cases: Zaccour, 'Parental Alienation in Quebec Custody Litigation' (2018), 30 Can. J. Fam. L. — https://www.canlii.org/en/commentary/doc/2018CanLIIDocs10698
Zaccour, 'Does Domestic Violence Disappear from Parental Alienation Cases? Five Lessons from Quebec' (2021), 33 Can. J. Fam. L. 301 — https://commons.allard.ubc.ca/can-j-fam-l/vol33/iss2/8/
Zaccour, 'Parental alienation: The one-size-fits-none theory,' Law360 Canada — https://www.law360.ca/ca/articles/1829261/
NAWL FEWO brief, 'Banning Parental Alienation Accusations in Family Court' (2024) — https://nawl.ca/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/NAWL-FEWO-brief-PA-EN.pdf
Lead spokesperson, January 2024 NAWL-led 250+-organisation coalition to ban PA accusations
practitioners.hook: Single most-published Canadian critique-camp lawyer on PA — Canadian counterpart to Joan Meier. Quebec-data-driven; opens Ontario and Quebec doors simultaneously.
practitioners.notes: Ontario-barred, not Quebec-barred despite Quebec-data corpus.

Co-presenter (with Prof. Peter Jaffe) of free e-learning courses on parent-child contact problems / alienation
Member, Ontario Domestic Violence Death Review Committee
practitioners.primary_contact: Luke's Place intake https://lukesplace.ca/ ; secondary via https://pamelacross.ca/contact/
practitioners.hook: Highest-profile DV-frame critique-camp lawyer in Ontario; J.A. Corry (Queen's Law), Laura Legge (LSO), Guthrie (LFO), and AG Victim Services awards.

Brief on Bill C-78 (Divorce Act amendments) to House of Commons Justice Committee

2022 Dena Moyal Distinguished Service Award (AFCC Ontario)

methodology_note: Final count 14 entries (13 living practitioners + 1 retired judge). Stance distribution among 13 living practitioners: recognition 6 (Niman, Wowk, Ludmer, Colman, MacLean, Benmor); critique 3 (Zaccour, Cross, Neilson); middle 5 (Bala, Birnbaum, Amissah-Ocran, McCarty, Pinsky). All firm / faculty URLs verified live as of 2026-05-25. Where year of call, KC year or specific case citations could not be confirmed from a public primary source they are flagged 'not publicly confirmed' inline. Quality over completeness — Steve Benmor and Lawrence Pinsky lack standalone PA stance sources and are tier 3 with that gap flagged; Charles Amissah-Ocran and Elizabeth McCarty are tier 3 institutional/case-record entries only.
